other_observers: Greg Mihalik was the first to find and identify this bird earlier same morning; we then viewed and listened to the bird together
duration: 1 hour overall as it came in and out of view or sang
description: Clear view of characteristic black hood with yellow face, breast and belly with olive green back.The bird was singing frequently both from high perches (50ft. plus) and lower branches.  It was foraging along the foundation and gutter of an older building along with numerous Yellow-rumped Warblers.  A couple times it worked a circuit around the immediate area and briefly down to the river.  It would be quiet for up to about 20 minutes but at least once sang continuously for 10 minutes.
photographer: Photographer: Sally Waterhouse, Sally Waterhouse, Sally Waterhouse
